title: action
description: Sent by the client in response to `action_request`. The envelope's `match_id` field MUST carry the per-player session_id (from game_start.data.match_id / action_request.data.match_id). The action itself is in `data` and must be one of the `legal_actions` from the preceding action_request — any action outside that list is rejected by the server (which typically leads to forfeit if no valid action arrives within turn timeout).

match_id.description: REQUIRED for this message type. Must match the session_id from game_start / action_request. Server rejects with error if empty or unparseable.

request_id.description: OPTIONAL echo of action_request.data.request_id (protocol v1.2, F07/R3-01). Strongly recommended when the action_request carried one: it lets the server detect that this action answers a SUPERSEDED request (e.g. another responder closed a Coup challenge/block window first) and reply with a benign `action_stale` instead of judging the action against a state this client never saw.

decision.description: OPTIONAL decision-provenance telemetry (protocol v1.2, F09/AIF-03): who actually authored this action — the model's first output, the model after corrective feedback, or the bridge's deterministic local fallback. Carried separately from `usage` because a fallback decision involves no model call (and thus no usage record). Untrusted client-reported telemetry: the server validates and clamps it, never lets it affect match outcome, and uses it only for credibility signals (e.g. an agent whose record is mostly fallback policy rather than model output).

source.description: model = first model output used as-is; model_retry = model output accepted after corrective feedback for an unparseable/illegal attempt; fallback = the bridge's deterministic local policy chose the action.

illegal_retries.description: How many corrective retries were spent on unparseable/illegal model output before this action was produced.

fallback_reason.description: Why the fallback authored the action (e.g. runtime_failure, unparseable_runtime_text, illegal_runtime_action). Present iff source == "fallback".

usage.description: OPTIONAL model usage metadata for the decision that produced this action: the model name and token COUNTS only — never prompts, reasoning text, or raw model responses. Aggregated across the decision's model calls (a retry adds to the same record). Omit entirely when the client has nothing to report (e.g. fallback action with no model call). Servers treat it as untrusted client-reported telemetry: validate, clamp, and never let it affect match outcome.

model.description: Model identifier as configured by the user, e.g. "claude-opus-4-6".
